concept: 800-VDC rack power-path protection: integrated solid-state breaker + precharge/inrush control + insulation monitoring + hot-swap sequencing unit for AI racks and power sidecars
named_buyer_examples: Alibaba datacenter power suppliers e.g. Shenzhen Autrans (CN)
painful_job: US: 800VDC racks specified by NVIDIA/OCP have no converged DC fault-interruption, precharge, grounding, or hot-swap solution (L02-043, L02-044; SSCB still research-stage L08-017, L08-001/003/004). CN: Huawei-ecosystem trade engineering flags 800V DC protection, grounding, hot-plug, and inlet-node oscillation as unresolved integration pain (L02-054); China HVDC datacenter penetration was only ~12% vs 78% UPS, so the conversion wave is ahead, not behind (L02-048).
product: A shelf-format 800V/250-500A hybrid solid-state protection-and-sequencing unit: <100 us fault clearing, arc-signature detection (L08-018/019/020/021 methods), managed precharge, IMD, and blind-mate hot-swap logic, pre-certified to IEC 62477-1 (L02-112). Imports from merged duplicates: fleet-wide DC arc-signature analytics with a UL/IEC listing-path emphasis (from A-01); synthesized-virtual-impedance active bus damping and capacitor-health (ripple/ESR) telemetry (from B-03); arc rate-of-change dataset moat built from accumulated fault-signature libraries (from E-01).
frontier_vision: Become the de facto 'circuit-breaker layer' for all DC-native infrastructure: 800V racks, 1500V sidecars, MVDC campus feeders, EV megawatt charging, and DC industrial parks.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: publish comparative SSCB topology benchmark from lab work; join OCP power workstream; file 2-3 provisional patents on combined precharge+breaking+arc-discrimination. 2027: decisive brassboard experiment (below). 2028: second-gen unit tested in a partner rack lab (integrator MOU, no company needed); UL/IEC pre-compliance data. 2029: design-in discussions with 2 US integrators and 1 CN ODM via licensing LOIs; refresh 2029 market/pricing evidence before incorporation.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: 800VDC ramps 2027-2029 with ad hoc protection; by 2030 hyperscalers demand standardized, certified protection at gigawatt-campus scale while DC breakers remain unconverged (L08-001/003/004/017) - a component vacuum a focused entrant can own.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: post-2027 NVIDIA Kyber/Rubin-Ultra 800VDC production architecture scaling into 2030-2034 gigawatt campus buildouts (L02-043) plus OCP Mount Diablo spec revisions institutionalizing DC power shelves (L02-044). CN: implementation cycle of the April 2026 national AI-energy action plan (Guo-Neng-Fa-Ke-Ji [2026] No.34, L14-036) and conversion of the historically low (~12%) HVDC penetration base in Chinese datacenters (L02-048).
competition_outlook_2030: US 2030: Eaton/Vertiv/Schneider integrated shelves, ABB/Siemens industrial DC breakers, 1-2 funded SSCB startups. CN 2030: Huawei in-house, Delta China, Autrans (L02-049), Innoscience-device-based designs (L02-050). Window: incumbents sell whole shelves; a certified merchant protection layer that all shelf vendors can embed is unoccupied.
timing_window_risk: Export-control separability: good - protection electronics are conventional industrial power electronics (largely EAR99); China variant can use domestic GaN/SiC (Innoscience, L02-050) built by a licensed CN entity while the US entity serves OCP customers; main risks are hyperscaler vertical integration, UL/IEC certification timeline, and any future US controls on high-performance power semiconductors. 2026 specs/prices must be refreshed in 2029.
extreme_edge: Sub-100-microsecond bidirectional fault interruption at 800V/500A in a hot-swappable shelf form factor, with arc discrimination that avoids nuisance trips at 1MW-rack transient levels.
first_experiment: Q2 2027: 800V/250A hybrid SSCB + precharge/hot-swap brassboard; demonstrate <100 us clearing into inductive fault, 10,000 hot-swap cycles, arc-signature discrimination on emulated rack bus; IEC 62477-1 pre-compliance test plan.
secondary_markets: TW: ODM co-design and manufacturing wedge - Delta and the Taipei 800VDC ecosystem build the racks this unit slots into (L02-037, L02-046) [from E-01]
JP: SiC device supply and co-qualification with ROHM-class 750V/1200V parts (L02-051) [from E-01]
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-01 (fleet arc-signature analytics, UL/IEC listing path), B-03 (virtual-impedance damping, capacitor ESR/ripple telemetry), B-04 (CN protection-stack variant; unique damping/telemetry content already covered), E-01 (arc-ROC dataset moat; TW Delta/COMPUTEX and JP ROHM supply wedges - best secondary-market logic in the cluster). China leg unchanged: already independently evidenced (L02-048/050/054, L14-036). Standing kill trigger: spec absorption by NVIDIA/OCP.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical 800V protection. Genuine independent US/CN demand legs; absorb A-01 fleet analytics, B-03 damping, E-01 ROC dataset + TW/JP wedges. Spec-absorption by NVIDIA/OCP is the standing kill trigger.

concept: Rack-level pulsed-load energy buffer: electrolytic-free 800V DC-link module (film caps + GaN partial-power converter) that damps GPU load transients and bus oscillation
painful_job: US: multi-stage conversion and DC-link electrolytic capacitors are leading converter failure mechanisms exactly as rack power density rises (L02-010, L02-043). CN: Chinese trade engineering explicitly names inlet-node voltage oscillation and hot-plug transients at 800V as unresolved (L02-054); AI-rack pulsed loads amplify both.
product: A 30-100 kW shelf module: metallized-film energy bank + GaN partial-power bidirectional converter providing 10:1 transient smoothing, active bus damping, and ride-through, replacing distributed electrolytic banks; telemetry for capacitor prognostics.
frontier_vision: Standard 'power shock absorber' for every pulsed-load electrical system: AI racks, EV megawatt chargers, pulsed industrial tools, and grid-edge nodes - the missing buffer layer between stochastic loads and clean sources.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: characterize GPU-rack pulse spectra from published OCP/NVIDIA material; simulate buffer sizing; provisional patents on partial-power buffer control. 2027: decisive demo (below). 2028: 100kW prototype in partner lab; reliability model vs electrolytic baseline (L02-010). 2029: integrator design-in LOIs; refresh competitive landscape (BBU vendors, Vicor-class modules). FIX additions: 2026-27 quantified cost-parity model vs distributed electrolytic banks and vs BBU shelves (the buffer must win on $/kW-cycle and reliability, not novelty); hard 2028 gate: one integrator evaluation agreement.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: 2027-2029 800V deployments will surface oscillation/reliability failures at fleet scale; by 2030 buyers will retrofit and spec-in buffering, before any standard component category exists.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: OCP power-shelf spec iterations for 600kW-1MW racks (L02-043/044) forcing a defined ride-through/buffer function 2030-2032. CN: AI-energy action plan efficiency push (L14-036) plus the Chinese HVDC conversion wave (L02-048) creating a parallel retrofit cycle.
competition_outlook_2030: US: Vicor-class dense modules (L02-113), BBU vendors (SiC BBUs already shipping, L02-051), PSU incumbents adding capacitance the crude way. CN: Huawei in-house, Delta China (L02-037). Facility-scale rotating buffers (Piller, L16-052) solve a different layer and validate the pain.
timing_window_risk: Commoditization risk is the main threat: if NVIDIA specs an in-rack buffer and Delta/Vicor ship it by 2029, the window narrows - kill trigger. Export separability good (industrial module, dual-entity manufacturable with local devices). Refresh 2026 rack-power assumptions (TDP/flow figures are inconsistent even now, L14-044) before committing capital. Codified kill trigger (FIX): if OCP/NVIDIA specs an in-rack buffer function or Delta/Vicor-class vendors ship an equivalent product by end-2029, fold this seed into P3R2-C-01 as its buffer/ride-through option and stop standalone investment.
extreme_edge: Handling megawatt-per-millisecond load slew at 800V with film-capacitor energy density via partial-power conversion, at electrolytic-bank cost parity.
first_experiment: 2027: 30kW brassboard buffer; reproduce published 800V inlet-oscillation profile and demonstrate 10:1 transient smoothing + active damping; 1,000h accelerated film-cap vs electrolytic reliability data.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: end-2029 fold-into-C-01 kill trigger codified; electrolytic-bank/BBU cost-parity quantification and 2028 integrator-eval gate added.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Clean mechanism; lives one spec revision from absorption.

concept: Medium-voltage solid-state transformer power block (2.4 MW class) converting 10-35 kV directly to 800 VDC for AI campuses, engineered against the IEC 60076 standards gap
primary_customer_archetype: datacenter developers, electrical EPCs, power-shelf OEMs
named_buyer_examples: OCP hyperscalers standardizing SST power units (US)
datacenter EPCs buying GE Vernova-class electrification packages (US)
Chinese hyperscaler campuses under the AI-energy action plan (CN)
painful_job: US: OCP Mount Diablo explicitly standardizes SST-based power units next to transformer-rectifier units (L02-044), but a decade of ARPA-E-funded MV SST work has not produced a certified deployable block (L02-033, L02-034, L02-036). CN: Chinese datacenters pioneered rack HVDC (L02-048) but lack an MV-to-800VDC stage; the 2026 AI-energy action plan pushes high-efficiency architectures (L14-036).
product: A modular 2.4 MW MVDC/MVAC-to-800VDC SST brick: SiC cell-stack, HF transformer with insulation coordination designed to close the documented IEC 60076 test mismatch (L02-036, L02-001), multiport-ready (L02-107), sold as a certified building block to shelf/skid OEMs rather than as a turnkey substation.
frontier_vision: Replace line-frequency transformer + rectifier chains everywhere power quality and footprint matter: datacenter campuses, EV megawatt hubs, industrial DC parks, and eventually distribution-grid nodes.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: Pre-company scope strictly limited (FIX): HF-insulation/partial-discharge dataset work and standards-body seats only - no converter-product development. 2026-28: university/consortium work (DC-GRIDS-adjacent, L02-034) on HF insulation + partial-discharge dataset targeting the standards gap; IEC/IEEE working-group participation so the 2029-2031 standard revision matches the design; publish; patents on insulation/cell-bypass. 2028 HARD GATE: demonstrable IEC 60076-24-class standard progress (committee draft or later) PLUS an OCP SST procurement signal (spec revision or hyperscaler RFI naming SST power units); if either is absent, re-batch this seed to the wildcard pool and do not raise or spend against the $25-60M v1 plan. 2028: 100kW cell-string experiment (below) only if the gate passes. 2029: LOI with one US hyperscaler pilot program and one CN integrator licensing conversation.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: The SST blocker is certification, not physics (L02-036); whoever holds a certification-ready design when OCP-era buyers scale 1MW racks in 2030-2033 wins a category incumbents have repeatedly failed to open despite 10+ years of funding (L02-033/034).
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: OCP Mount Diablo SST-power-unit track maturing into procurement specs 2030-2032 (L02-044) amid continuing multi-billion-dollar datacenter electrification orders (L08-033). CN: AI-energy action plan implementation plus green-power direct-connection framework (L14-036) favoring direct MV-DC coupling in new campuses.
competition_outlook_2030: US: Eaton/Schneider/Vertiv shelf incumbents, GE Vernova at grid scale (L02-115), Heron-class SST startups. CN: Huawei, Delta (L02-037), State-Grid-adjacent suppliers. Expect 2-3 credible SST entrants by 2030; certification depth and OCP alignment are the moats.
timing_window_risk: Highest-capex seed in this batch; standards timing may slip (the same lag that blocked incumbents). Export separability moderate: MV SiC stacks to CN may face controls; plan CN-entity build with domestic SiC. Kill if no certified MW-class field pilot by end-2033. All 2026 program facts (DC-GRIDS, OCP roadmap) must be re-verified in 2029. FIX: the 2028 standards+procurement gate is binding; a blocked certification category must not carry $25-60M capex.
extreme_edge: MV-to-LVDC conversion at >99% availability with HF isolation at 10-35kV - the hardest live insulation/thermal co-design problem in commercial power electronics.
first_experiment: 2028: 100kW MV cell-string with HF transformer; partial-discharge and thermal-cycling dataset against IEC 60076-24-relevant test regimes; publish certification-gap analysis with a standards body.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: hard 2028 gate on IEC 60076-24-class standards progress plus an OCP SST procurement signal; pre-company scope restricted to HF-insulation/PD dataset and standards seats; re-batch-to-wildcard rule if the gate fails; confidence lowered medium->low (schedule-fragile category).
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Certification-first thesis is a real insight against the atlas's MV-SST negative finding; schedule-fragile.

concept: PFAS-free pumped two-phase direct-to-chip cooling loop: hermetic, fluid-agnostic evaporator/condenser subsystem co-qualified with low-GWP non-PFAS working fluids
named_buyer_examples: Chinese liquid-cooling system vendors qualifying to T/CIEP 0263-2025 (CN)
Chinese AI-datacenter operators under GB 40879 PUE limits (CN)
painful_job: US: two-phase cooling is the path past single-phase cold plates for 3-5kW chips (L14-010, L14-001/002/003), but 3M's PFAS exit removed the qualified fluid family while the AIM Act tightens HFCs 2029-2033 (L14-033) - vendors have no fluid-plus-loop combination they can bank. CN: GB 40879 pushes PUE to <=1.25/1.2 (L14-035), T/CIEP 0263-2025 codifies liquid cooling (L14-039), and Chinese reviews call phase-change cooling the trajectory (L14-029), with the same global fluid vacuum.
product: Primary variant (merged from P3R2-B-01): a sealed negative-pressure (subatmospheric) two-phase loop - saturation temperature set by vacuum setpoint; seal failures leak air inward (vacuum-decay detected) rather than coolant onto boards; PFAS-free by design on water or low-GWP fluids (ITRI-class low-pressure results, L14-053). Second moat: the fluid-menu qualification dataset - cold plate + micro-pump + condenser + charge management co-qualified across a menu of non-PFAS, low-GWP fluids. Sold to ODM/CDU vendors as a drop-in >2kW/chip module with fluid-lifetime warranty data.
frontier_vision: Own the two-phase thermal layer for the 5kW-chip era across datacenters, power electronics, EV fast-charging, and radar - fluid-chemistry-independent, so every future regulatory shock becomes our upgrade cycle.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: fluid screening matrix with a chemical partner (Chemours/Honeywell-class), building the post-Novec qualification dataset the literature lacks (L14-022); patents on charge management and fluid-agnostic evaporator design. 2027: decisive 2kW demo (below). 2028: 6-month reliability run + hyperscaler lab evaluation. 2029: ODM design-in LOI; refresh fluid regulatory status (AIM Act step, EU F-gas) before launch.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Regulatory clocks (AIM Act 30% step 2029, L14-033; EU F-gas, L14-034) collide with 3-5kW chips right at 2030; incumbents bet on single-phase water or on discontinued PFAS fluids, leaving a certified PFAS-free two-phase loop unowned at launch.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: EPA AIM Act HFC allowance step-down to 30% of baseline for 2029-2033 (L14-033) plus next-gen accelerator TDPs >4kW (L14-046 context, L14-010). CN: GB 40879 revision enforcement (PUE <=1.25/1.2, L14-035) and T/CIEP 0263-2025 adoption cycle (L14-039) as new-campus acceptance criteria.
competition_outlook_2030: US: ZutaCore (waterless two-phase, $100M+ C, L14-046), Accelsius, LiquidStack/Trane (L14-051), JetCool/Flex microconvective (L14-045), Motivair/Schneider CDUs. CN: Envicool (Deschutes CDU vendor list, L14-043), Huawei in-house. Differentiator: fluid-agnostic qualification data, not another proprietary fluid bet.
timing_window_risk: Fluid availability is the binding risk - if no non-PFAS two-phase fluid reaches datacenter-scale qualification by 2031, single-phase water wins another cycle (kill trigger). Export separability good: thermal hardware is uncontrolled; CN entity can localize manufacturing. Do not rely on the unconfirmed Chinese '100% liquid-cooling mandate' (contradicted by primary text, L14-036). FIX: the 2031 fluid-availability kill trigger is retained and extended to cover the subatmospheric-water variant.
extreme_edge: Stable flow-boiling heat removal above 500 W/cm2 hotspot flux in a sealed, orientation-tolerant loop with non-engineered-fluid chemistries.
first_experiment: 2027: 2kW cold-plate pumped two-phase loop running two candidate non-PFAS fluids; map heat-flux/stability envelope vs Novec-era published baselines; 1,000h sealed-loop degradation assay.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: adopted B-01's negative-pressure architecture as the primary loop mechanism (defensible-by-physics wedge) with the fluid-menu qualification dataset as second moat; 2031 fluid-availability kill trigger retained; confidence high->medium pending the mechanism demo. B-01 remains the CN-primary canonical; C-04 carries the US+CN dual variant.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): As written a positioning play vs funded two-phase startups; regulatory triggers genuinely independent per country.

concept: Standards-grade thermal emulation and conformance metrology: programmable multi-kW chip-scale thermal test vehicles and rack-scale load emulators for liquid-cooling qualification
primary_customer_archetype: CDU/cold-plate vendors, ODMs, datacenter commissioning agents
named_buyer_examples: The 7+ vendors building CDUs to Google's Deschutes spec (Boyd, CoolerMaster, Delta, Envicool, Nidec, nVent, Vertiv) (US spec ecosystem)
Chinese liquid-cooling vendors certifying to T/CIEP 0263-2025 (CN)
Chinese datacenter operators proving GB 40879 PUE compliance (CN)
fluid makers (Chemours/Honeywell archetype) qualifying post-PFAS fluids (from A-15)
Chinese certification/inspection labs adopting T/CIEP 0263-2025 (from B-02)
painful_job: US: cooling vendors design against inconsistent secondary-source thermal specs - even GB200-class TDP/flow figures conflict across sources and NVIDIA's public page does not resolve them (L14-044) - so over/under-engineering risk lands on CDU vendors building to open specs (L14-043). CN: T/CIEP 0263-2025 and tightening PUE limits create conformance/acceptance-test demand with no standard test hardware (L14-039, L14-035).
product: Calibrated silicon/vapor-chamber thermal test vehicles (0.2-5 kW, programmable hotspot maps), manifold/rack emulators to 2 MW class, and traceable test protocols for liquid/two-phase cooling acceptance - the 'thermal dynamometer' for the industry. Merged extensions: precision flow/enthalpy calorimetry benches specified to <=1% energy-balance closure and accelerated post-PFAS fluid-qualification rigs (materials compatibility, dielectric aging) (from A-15); automated clause-by-clause conformance reporting against T/CIEP 0263-2025 plus a sell-to-certification-labs channel (from B-02).
frontier_vision: Become the JEDEC-era-defining metrology vendor for liquid-cooled computing: every cold plate, CDU, fluid, and TIM qualified on our vehicles, with the reference dataset feeding standards bodies in US, CN, and OCP.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: build first 1.2kW programmable test vehicle in a university lab; publish an open cross-vendor cold-plate comparison to establish methodology authority; engage OCP cooling workstream and Chinese standards ecosystem via an academic partner. 2028: rack-emulator pilot with one CDU vendor; protocol whitepaper. 2029: pre-orders/LOIs from 3 vendors; refresh chip-power roadmaps (2026 TDP figures will be stale).
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Cooling hardware revenue is exploding (Vertiv +50% liquid-cooling orders, $15B backlog, L14-048) while test methodology lags chips by two generations; by 2030, warranty and PUE-compliance stakes force third-party-grade qualification - a picks-and-shovels position with tiny capex.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: OCP-driven multi-vendor CDU/cold-plate proliferation (L14-043) plus liquid-cooling M&A integration waves (L14-045, L14-051) creating qualification bottlenecks 2030-2032. CN: T/CIEP 0263-2025 conformance adoption and GB 40879 PUE acceptance testing for national-hub datacenters (L14-039, L14-035).
competition_outlook_2030: US: in-house dummy-load rigs, thermal test houses; no dominant merchant vendor today. CN: institute labs; ITRI in Taiwan builds capability (L14-053) and NTT runs a verification hub in Japan (L14-050) - institutional analogs, not product companies. First credible product vendor can set the method.
timing_window_risk: Modest market-size ceiling is the real risk (instrumentation TAM), mitigated by consumables/protocol licensing; export separability excellent (test equipment, dual-entity trivial). If OCP or JEDEC ships a full method + reference hardware by 2030 without us, differentiation collapses - monitor via standards participation.
extreme_edge: Emulating 1,000 W/cm2-class transient hotspot maps with traceable calorimetry at rack scale - metrology fidelity nobody currently sells.
first_experiment: Q4 2026-2027: 1.2kW programmable thermal test vehicle with 16-zone hotspot control; blind round-robin of 3 commercial cold plates; publish method + variance data.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-15 (post-PFAS fluid-qualification rigs; <=1%-closure calorimetry spec; AIM Act/EU F-gas demand ids L14-033/034) and B-02 (T/CIEP 0263-2025 clause-scripted reporting; certification-lab channel; corrosion/biofouling protocol source L14-028). CN leg unchanged - already US+CN with T/CIEP-0263/GB-40879 evidence.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical thermal metrology. Documented spec chaos as demand driver; independent US/CN legs; tiny capex; TAM ceiling honestly flagged. Absorb A-15 and B-02.

concept: Tailored-voltage-waveform RF bias platform: arbitrary-waveform bias generator + adaptive matching subsystem productizing ion-energy-distribution control for plasma etch/deposition tools
named_buyer_examples: US/Western tool OEM programs currently served by Advanced Energy/MKS (US)
painful_job: US: tailored-voltage-waveform biasing for atomic-scale etch is a live 2024-2026 frontier (L06-009/010/011/027) not yet an off-the-shelf subsystem; Advanced Energy's new platforms are still 'early pilot/early production' (L06-039), evidencing unmet pull. CN: RF power components are <12% localized (L06-054) while Chinese tool OEMs grow 40-55%/yr (L06-048) on a fragile two-supplier domestic base (Hengyunchang concentration, L06-042/043; Injet, L06-044).
product: A waveform-programmable bias generator (kHz-MHz synthesis, sub-ns edge control) with co-designed fast adaptive match (L06-001/002/003) and IEDF-based closed loop, sold as a bolt-on subsystem to tool OEMs; CN product line limited to >=28nm-class applications.
frontier_vision: The 'waveform is the process' company: programmable-energy plasma control across etch, deposition, implant surface engineering, and industrial plasma chemistry - displacing fixed-frequency RF the way arbitrary waveform generators displaced signal generators.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: university plasma-lab collaboration replicating TVW IEDF control on a 300mm ICP testbed; publish; patents on match-network/waveform co-optimization. 2027: decisive experiment (below). 2028: alpha unit in one OEM process lab (JDA-style, pre-company). 2029: two design-in LOIs (one US-facing, one CN >=28nm); export-control legal review completed before incorporation.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: TVW is transitioning from papers to products exactly now; incumbents productize slowly inside legacy generator architectures, and China's localization drive guarantees a second, independent buyer set (L06-054) - both windows peak around 2030 when sub-2nm (US) and mature-node capacity (CN) expansions collide with process-control limits.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: next-generation logic/memory etch requirements pushing OEM adoption of waveform bias in HVM 2030-2033 (trajectory evidenced by L06-039 platform ramp + L06-009 industry co-authorship). CN: continued RF-component localization push from <12% base (L06-054) with AMEC/NAURA capex cycles (L06-048) and domestic-supplier fragility (L06-042/043).
competition_outlook_2030: US 2030: Advanced Energy (Everest/EVOS/NavX generations), MKS, Comet Synertia (L06-049), TRUMPF Huettinger (L01-049). CN 2030: Hengyunchang, Injet, plus new IPO-funded entrants. Merchant TVW-specialist niche likely still open because incumbents monetize installed generator bases.
timing_window_risk: Export-control separability is the defining constraint: US rules restrict advanced-node (<14/16nm) equipment to China - structure as US entity (leading-edge) + CN entity (>=28nm-class variants for AMEC/NAURA), with clean IP partition; risk that controls broaden to all RF subsystems by 2030 would cut the CN side (seed then defaults to US-only). Re-verify control scope and China localization percentages in 2029 (2026 figures will be stale).
extreme_edge: Closed-loop synthesis of ion-energy distributions at the wafer within microseconds - turning plasma physics into a programmable instrument.
first_experiment: 2027: TVW bias + adaptive match on a 300mm university ICP testbed; demonstrate programmable IEDF narrowing vs sinusoidal baseline and <10 us re-match under waveform switching.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (REJECT): Import the US/CN two-entity export partition into A-10 as company structure.

concept: Low-ripple active-front-end rectification retrofits for electrochemical plants: IGBT/MMC power-quality upgrades that recover the documented 5-14% specific-energy penalty of thyristor supplies
primary_customer_archetype: electrolyzer plant operators, electrowinning tank houses
named_buyer_examples: DOE H2Hubs projects (HyVelocity Gulf Coast, MachH2) (US)
US copper electrowinning operators (rectifier-quality lever per vendor evidence)
painful_job: US: hydrogen projects financed under H2Hubs carry 15-20-year opex sensitivity to rectifier ripple - the literature documents up to ~14% specific-energy and ~13% loss penalties for six-pulse thyristor supplies vs transistor-based conversion (L11-016/017/019/020/021), and the same lever exists in electrowinning (L11-045, L11-026/027). CN: flagship plants underperform - Kuqa reportedly ran at a fraction of nameplate (L11-049) - while alkaline mega-tenders (L11-050/051) install cost-optimized thyristor rectification at GW scale.
product: Containerized active-front-end/MMC rectifier retrofit skids (5-50 MW class) with ripple/harmonic control tuned to stack electrochemistry, plus measurement-based performance contracts; stack-vendor-neutral. Merged extensions: per-cell/per-stack telemetry with a financier-grade measurement-and-verification (M&V) protocol backing the performance contracts (from A-19); electrochemistry-aware, degradation-weighted current scheduling that maps renewable variability into stack-safe trajectories (from B-13).
frontier_vision: Precision DC as a product: the power-quality layer for all electrochemistry - hydrogen, electrowinning, chlor-alkali, battery formation - capturing the efficiency margin chemistry vendors leave on the table.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: instrumented ripple-vs-degradation study on a pilot electrolyzer with a university/RTO partner (extends L11-001/003/004/021/031); publish the retrofit-economics model. 2027: decisive side-by-side experiment (below). 2028: MW-scale retrofit pilot MOU with one US project developer and one CN EPC via academic consortium. 2029: refresh policy (45V status), tender prices, and Chinese rectifier-vendor landscape before incorporation. Beachhead sequencing (from A-19): US copper-electrowinning tankhouses first (policy-independent energy lever), H2Hubs second.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Stack overcapacity (China ~2x demand, L11-035/051) makes stacks a commodity while electricity is the cost driver - exactly the environment where a 5-14% energy lever sells; by 2030 both markets have enough installed thyristor base to retrofit and enough new-build to spec-in.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: H2Hubs Phase-2 construction disbursements and commissioning 2027-2032 (L11-032) plus electrowinning energy-cost programs. CN: post-2030 second-wave green-H2 base buildouts and retrofit of underperforming first-wave plants (L11-049), with tender cadence evidenced by L11-050/051.
competition_outlook_2030: US 2030: ABB/Siemens/Ingeteam rectifier lines (industry incumbents - names not in atlas, flagged for P4), OEM-bundled power (Siemens Energy L11-040, Accelera L11-041). CN 2030: Sungrow - simultaneously buyer and formidable power-electronics competitor (L11-051) - plus domestic rectifier makers. Wedge: stack-neutral performance contracts and electrochemistry-specific ripple control.

concept: Real-time HIL compliance-test systems for DC protection and inverter-based-resource ride-through: the certification bench for the PRC-029/HVDC era
named_buyer_examples: US IBR owners/vendors facing NERC PRC-029-1 compliance (US)
XJ Electric-class converter-valve/protection suppliers needing factory acceptance testing (CN)

launch_2030_timing_thesis: Compliance testing demand lags rule-making by 3-5 years - PRC-029 (2025) and the HVDC buildout put peak certification demand exactly in 2030-2034, while incumbent HIL vendors underserve DC-fault-specific physics.

competition_outlook_2030: RTDS/OPAL-RT-class real-time simulator incumbents (industry names, flag for P4 - not in atlas), in-house rigs at XJ/NARI (CN) and OEM labs. Wedge: DC-fault fidelity (microsecond traveling-wave physics) + turnkey compliance scripting.

Risk: incumbent simulator vendors add DC-fault packs first (monitor 2027-2029); regulatory timelines can slip. Kill if incumbents close the gap before first sales (decision 2033).
extreme_edge: Reproducing <1ms DC fault clearing physics - traveling waves, zero-crossing-free interruption, converter interactions - with hardware in the loop at certification fidelity.
first_experiment: 2027: FPGA real-time DC-grid emulator reproducing published multi-terminal fault records; blind test of two published protection algorithms; publish fidelity benchmark.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (REJECT): Import CN factory-acceptance-test license market and State Grid test-institute channel into E-14.

concept: HTS magnet quench detection and protection electronics: fast multi-modal detection + energy-extraction control for no-insulation and insulated REBCO coils
painful_job: US: HTS quench detection is unsolved at multi-tesla/multi-megajoule scale - high minimum quench energy defeats conventional voltage-tap schemes, forcing conservative no-insulation designs with degraded ramp/control (L03-004-008, L03-018); magnet-as-product sales (L03-035) make protection a warranty item. frontier_vision: Every high-field magnet - fusion, NMR, accelerators, medical - ships with our protection layer; the trust infrastructure that lets HTS magnets be insured, financed, and mass-produced.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: multi-modal detection physics on university test coils; publish latency benchmarks vs voltage taps. 2027: decisive NI-coil experiment (below). 2028: characterization pilot with one US magnet program. 2029: subsystem LOI; export-control review for any CN engagement (fusion-magnet tech sensitivity).
launch_2030_timing_thesis: SPARC-generation results 2026-27 trigger follow-on magnet production in the US and accelerate China's domestic program; both scale coil counts 10-100x into 2030-2034 - protection electronics scale per-coil, and no merchant supplier exists.

Kill if detection latency targets unmet on representative coils by 2033.
extreme_edge: Detecting a normal-zone birth releasing milliwatts inside a 100+ MJ magnet and acting within milliseconds - the highest-consequence anomaly-detection problem in applied superconductivity.
first_experiment: 2027: fiber+RF multi-modal quench detection on an instrumented NI REBCO test coil; demonstrate >=5x faster detection than voltage taps across 20 induced quenches.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (REJECT): Seed concedes CN leg may be export-blocked and invites re-batching; D-01 is the cleaner canonical.
